{{Quote|I will ''not'' permit the ship I designed nor my new ''factory'' to serve the Empire's evil!|Vors Voorhorian|<ref name="xwmts" />}}
'''Vors Voorhorian''' was an [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] who designed the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]]. After being arrested by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] following the [[Battle of Fresia|seizure]] of the X-wing prototypes by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] agents, he escaped and set up his own factory on a remote world. Fearful of Imperial retribution, he set up several contingency plans securing his newly manufactured craft and even took to disguising himself as a used [[Starship/Legends|starship]] salesman to deter suspicion.

Following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Voorhorian was contacted by [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], who sought replacements for the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] lost in the battle. He obliged, although the Rebels inadvertently led Imperial starfighters to his secret factory. Luckily for him, his fighters were secure, and after a brief engagement with a squad of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]], the new ships were securely in Alliance hands. Voorhorian decided that he would be better served helping the Rebellion and upon the delivery of his craft to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], he remained on the forest [[Moon/Legends|moon]], at the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]]. His fighter would go on to serve the Alliance, and in turn the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], for many years.

==Biography==
===On the run from the Empire===
{{Quote|The designer of the X-wing, ''Vors Voorhorian'', ''escaped'' and set up production on a remote planet!|Leia Organa|<ref name="xwmts" />}}
In his work as an engineer for the [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]], Vors Voorhorian designed the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]], a space superiority craft.<ref name="xwmts" /> The prototypes for the craft eventually fell into the hands of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], stolen from the main Incom facility on [[Fresia/Legends|Fresia]].<ref name="eaw">''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''</ref> In retaliation, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] arrested Voorhorian<ref name="xwmts" /> and all the other Incom scientists who had developed the craft, and seized control of the main Incom facility on Fresia.<ref name="eaw" />

[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|Raymus Antilles]] was able to liberate the majority of the Incom staff,<ref name="eaw" /> and it is possible that Voorhorian was freed during the [[Space Battle in the Kessel Asteroid Field]] as well.<ref name="hc">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=955#15870004|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-11-01|time=12:47 PM|quote=Not a bad assumption, but not official until a Star Wars author decides they want to connect the events of Empire at War with the Star Wars Kids comics.}}</ref> While Incom was temporarily out of business due to the efforts of the Empire, Voorhorian set up his own [[Incom Factory|production facility]] on a remote planet. He was highly suspicious of Imperial spies, however, and when the factory had produced a full complement of X-wings, he had them moved to a derelict [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]] orbiting the planet. In their place, he created X-wing chassis and had them stored in the factory for display.<ref name="xwmts" />

===Contacting the Alliance===
{{Quote|Thank you for pointing out the one flaw in my ''disguise'', Captain Solo…for that, you may see the X-wing fighters your Rebellion so sorely ''needs!''<s></s>|Vors Voorhorian|<ref name="xwmts" />}}
The [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], while a significant victory for the Alliance, cost them most of their fleet of X-wings. Voorhorian was contacted by [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], who hoped to replenish their supply of the lost fighters, and had just come across a considerable amount of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Voorhorian gave Organa the coordinates of a beacon he had set up, which would transmit a message from "Gears Gilhooey," a local used starship salesman. The beacon would then transmit the coordinates of the planet, and self-destruct. Voorhorian was paranoid that the Empire would discover his location, and exact retribution on the citizens of the planet, just as they had done with Fresia. He had adopted the guise of "Gilhooey" to dissuade suspicion.<ref name="xwmts" />

[[File:VorsReveal.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Voorhorian reveals himself to the Rebels.]]

Shortly thereafter, Organa arrived on the planet with [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. Voorhorian, as Gilhooey, approached the group, and though they initially ignored his falsified sales pitch, Solo noticed that Gilhooey wore an X-wing cog for a ring, just as Voorhorian supposedly did. Not given the chance to explain himself, Voorhorian found himself in the grasp of Solo, who attempted to interrogate him. Voorhorian quickly turned the tables, however, and was able to throw the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] to the ground. At that moment, Voorhorian chose to reveal himself and his hidden factory. After explaining the need for the deception, he began discussing terms with Organa, while Solo, Skywalker, and R2-D2 unloaded the credits from the Corellian's ship, the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']].<ref name="xwmts" />

During the discussions, R2-D2 discovered that the X-wings in the factories were just empty chassis, prompting Solo and Skywalker to demand answers. They fought Voorhorian's guards, and began questioning the Incom engineer. Though Organa already knew, Voorhorian explained his second ruse, and although Solo and Skywalker were satisfied, the factory fell under Imperial starfighter attack. A group of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]] had followed the ''Falcon'' since its exit from hyperspace, and had began razing the structures on the remote planet.<ref name="xwmts" />

Quickly fleeing the Imperial attack, Voorhorian boarded the ''Falcon'', while Skywalker put his [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] skills to use and halted the advance of the enemy fighters. Once in orbit, Voorhorian directed Captain Solo to the derelict transport and explained his reasoning as to why he had hidden the fighters in such an inconspicuous place. While the Rebels inspected the fighters, the Imperials attacked them once again. Solo was able to lure them away from the transport for a short while in the ''Falcon'', but Skywalker had a better long-term plan. He had Voorhorian divert power from the powerful batteries of the X-wings to the shields of the transport. Then, using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], Skywalker activated the shields just as the TIE Fighters flew within range, destroying them. The ''Falcon'', using its [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]], then towed the transport back to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], where the [[Great Temple/Legends|Rebel base]] was located.<ref name="xwmts" />

===Imperial spy===
[[File:VorsRogor.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Voorhorian confers with Lieutenant Rogor, actually an Imperial spy.]]
{{Quote|I must say, it's ''gratifying'' to see my ''X-wings'' in the hands of the ''Rebellion'', as they were ''intended'' to be…and it'll be even ''better'' seeing them fighting ''Imperial''—|Vors Voorhorian|<ref name="is" />}}
Voorhorian decided to help the Alliance, and upon reaching Yavin 4, he stayed instead of returning to the now-destroyed Incom factory. Along with Skywalker, he helped shuttle Rebel pilots to the transport in a modified Incom [[T-16 skyhopper/Legends|T-16 skyhopper]], so they could take the new X-wings down to the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]]. On one run, Voorhorian and Skywalker encountered the [[Bail Organa (CR90 corvette)|''Bail Organa'']], a ship of refugees from the destroyed planet of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. As the days went by, the Rebels anticipated an Imperial attack, and sure enough, their filing systems were sabotaged by a spy.<ref name="is" />

When Organa accused Captain Solo of being the spy, Voorhorian was in disbelief. He expressed his feelings to [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Rogor]], who was also apparently shocked, but believed it to be possible. Eventually, Rogor was the one revealed to be the spy, and he attempted to escape in one of Voorhorian's X-wings. However, it was not one of the new craft, and it had been drained of power. Although, eventually, the Rebels were forced to [[Evacuation of Yavin|evacuate from Yavin]], they were able to escape the Empire's grasp.<ref name="is" /> The X-wing would go on to be a mainstay in the Alliance fleet, and would even serve the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] consistently.<ref name="rotj">{{Film|VI}}</ref><ref name="xwa">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''</ref><ref name="de">''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
[[File:VorsHand.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The X-wing cog Voorhorian wore as a ring.]]
Vors Voorhorian was a proud man: he had the utmost faith in both his ships and his plans. Though he was loathe to boast, he was quick to applaud his own work, especially the design of the X-wing's power cell. He hated the Empire for what they had done to him and Incom, and wanted to see his craft in action against their agents. Despite this, he was afraid of it, and created several successful security devices to divert attention away from his operations.<ref name="is" /><ref name="xwmts" />

Voorhorian was a skilled actor, and he was able to easily transform himself into "Gears Gilhooey," a loud and simple man, which was a far cry from his quiet and passive demeanor. He was also a skilled unarmed combatant, and was able to defeat Han Solo. Voorhorian kept a constant reminder of his work on the X-wing: a cog from that ship's design that he wore as a ring. He would wear this even while in disguise, as a point of recognition for his potential clients.<ref name="xwmts" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vors Voorhorian was created by [[Mike W. Barr]], and first appeared in ''[[X-Wing Marks the Spot]]''. The character would go on to appear in the sequel, ''[[Imperial Spy]]''. Following this, no references were made to Voorhorian in ''Star Wars'' media, despite him being the designer of an iconic vehicle in the ''Star Wars'' franchise. The drought ended, however, in [[2007]], with the release of ''[[Star Wars: Complete Cross-Sections]]'', which mentions him as the designer of the X-wing.
